name: Refactor O(n^2) to O(n log n) — perf-gated
task: The function find_duplicates in /tmp/eval_perf/dupes.py is O(n^2) and too slow for large inputs. Rewrite it so that on N=100,000 elements it completes in under 1 second on this machine, while keeping the SAME signature and SAME output (values that appear more than once, in their first-occurrence order, each listed only once). The naive nested-loop approach must be replaced — think about hash-based or sort-based strategies.

validation.script: import importlib.util, time, random; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_perf/dupes.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); fd = mod.find_duplicates; assert fd([]) == []; assert fd([1,2,3]) == []; assert fd([1,2,1,3,2]) == [1,2], f'small got {fd([1,2,1,3,2])}'; random.seed(7); big = [random.randint(0, 50000) for _ in range(100000)]; t0 = time.monotonic(); out = fd(big); elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0; assert elapsed < 1.0, f'too slow: {elapsed:.2f}s (must be < 1s)'; from collections import Counter as _C; expected_set = {v for v, c in _C(big).items() if c > 1}; assert set(out) == expected_set, f'wrong values: missing {expected_set - set(out)}'; assert len(out) == len(set(out)), 'duplicates in output'; first_seen = {}; [first_seen.setdefault(v, i) for i, v in enumerate(big) if v in expected_set]; expected_order = sorted(expected_set, key=first_seen.get); assert out == expected_order, 'wrong order'; print('OK')
